eyebrow: Rare genetic diseases · for families and clinicians
subtitle: Find everything already known about your disease in one place: guidelines in plain language and with sources, doctors who genuinely know it, and clinical trials. Gathered so that what is already known reaches the family and the doctor in time.
knowTitle: I know what I'm looking for
knowDesc: Go straight to the disease page — a step-by-step "what to do" map (what to check, which doctors to look for, what to ask at the visit), with guidelines, trials, and foundations alongside.
searchPlaceholder: e.g. fibrous dysplasia, dysplazja włóknista, MAS, GNAS…
searchHint: Search in English, Latin, by gene name, or by symptom — spelling and typos are fine.
dontTitle: I don't know where to start
dontDescLead: Only have a symptom or a genetic test result? Type what you have — we'll show possible directions and which specialist to talk to.
symptomPlaceholder: e.g. café-au-lait spots  ·  or  GNAS c.601C>T
findsSub: In one place, for any rare disease — and if yours isn't here yet, you can commission a research run.

finds.title: Guidelines in plain language — and the full versions to print
finds.body: A short, understandable summary of what is known about the disease — with a link to the source behind every sentence. Alongside it, the full original documents to print and show your doctor.

finds.title: Doctors with real experience in this disease
finds.body: A rare disease is rare — even an excellent specialist may never have seen it. It's not about competence, it's about scale: we help you find the few who have hands-on experience with it, and sort them by distance from you.

finds.title: Therapies and clinical trials — including abroad
finds.body: Including those not reimbursed in your country but running at centers abroad — sometimes those are the ones that offer a real chance. We show what is recruiting right now and where.

finds.title: Foundations that can help
finds.body: The kind of support that can change a lot with these diagnoses — patient organizations and foundations focused on the specific disease.

honestFootnote: Honestly: this is an AI-written summary of official guidelines and the literature (with links to sources) — not an official guideline, and no one formally signs off on it; clinicians can rate it and flag concerns. Informational material, not a diagnosis.
diseasesSectionSub: This isn't the whole catalog — the newest diseases that have been through our research. You can commission any other in a minute.
newDiseaseSub: Commission an AI research run — the pipeline searches PubMed and the web, and finds doctors and trials. First results in ~10 minutes.
addTitle: Your disease isn't here? You can add it.
addSub: Commission an AI research run for any rare disease — if it isn't in the database, the pipeline searches PubMed and the web and adds it. Missing some information or a feature? Tell us too.
